name: the-sun-does-shine
description: Anthony Ray Hinton's 'The Sun Does Shine: How I Found Life and Freedom on Death Row' — a powerful memoir of 30 years on death row for a crime he didn't commit, revealing how he found hope, forgiveness, and humanity in the darkest place.
useCase: Understand wrongful convictions, find hope in hopeless circumstances, practice forgiveness, and learn about criminal justice reform.
knownRisks: Contains graphic descriptions of prison conditions and executions
May be emotionally difficult for readers affected by incarceration or the death penalty
Hinton's experience is one story — not representative of all death row experiences
